message:
perf(demo): Eliminate per-line allocations in case-insensitive search

Search Performance Optimization:
- Add `line_contains_ignore_case()` function that performs case-insensitive
  substring matching without any heap allocations
- Pre-compute lowercase query once before iterating over lines, avoiding
  a String allocation for every single line in the text

Implementation Details:
- Uses byte-level comparison with `to_ascii_lowercase()` on each byte
- Implements naive search window algorithm that slides query across line
- Early exit on first match for boolean contains check
- No allocation: operates directly on byte slices

Impact:
- Code Explorer: Previously allocated a new String per line during search
  (potentially thousands of lines)
- Shakespeare: Same issue with 100k+ lines of text causing massive
  allocation pressure during interactive search

This change eliminates O(n) allocations per keystroke during search,
significantly improving responsiveness for large documents while
maintaining identical search semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
